notes: Use the official FP8 Hugging Face checkpoint for JAX or HF-style workflows.
This is the canonical reference artifact for the Qwen large-model baseline.
Use the MLX community conversion for local Apple Silicon benchmarking.
This is a practical MLX-native equivalent of the same Qwen model family, not identical FP8 weights.
Use a GGUF quantization from this repository for llama.cpp runs.
Pick a quant level that fits the target machine and record the exact file name in benchmark metadata.
Use the hosted local Ollama model for large-model benchmarks.
Record the Ollama tag and local runtime version for reproducibility.
Use the official OpenAI open-weight checkpoint for HF-style or JAX-adjacent workflows.
This model requires the harmony response format according to the official model card.
Use the MLX community conversion derived from openai/gpt-oss-120b.
This is the MLX-native large-model choice for local Apple Silicon experiments.
Use the GGUF quantization family for llama.cpp.
The bartowski page recommends the MXFP4_MOE file for gpt-oss-120b.
Use the Ollama-hosted 120B tag for local benchmarking.
Ollama states it supports the MXFP4 format natively for gpt-oss.
